Other: 1710: At 5:10 PM the Rebel Steamer Nashville passed down the Solent, Capt Craven of the U. S. Steam Sloop Tuscarora being notified by the British Naval Authorities, through Capt Patey of the Royal British Navy that the Tuscarora would not be allowed to leave until the expiration of 24 hours. The British Steam Frigate Shannon, of 51 guns, being near the Tuscarora, got up steam on the appearance of the Nashville and ~~~ down the river for the apparent purpose of preventing the Tuscarora proceeding in chase of the Rebel steamer.

8AM: At 0210 they saw what they was Cadiz Light. Then, at 0600, they thought that light was one at the mouth of Rio Guadalquivir, which is probably the one at Punta del Perro. Then, at 0800, they think that the light was really at Cape Trafalgar. 8AM: cc NW. Land showing we were E of Cape Trafalgar.
8AM: I changed Baro 30.02 to 30.12. It is unlikely to drop 0.10 inches and then rise 0.20 inches with no wind or weather occurring.
